translated: I totally didn't expect 'UTOPIA MAGIC' to come up so early,
It feels more like a song you'd save for the encore, doesn't it?
But they performed it as the second song.
But it really fit the 'Music World' atmosphere perfectly.
It had such a theme park feel to it.
We were even saying... what was it again?
We talked about wanting to do it like that.
We were told to have the energy of parade performers, welcoming everyone,
and to use really big, wide movements.
We all talked about it together.
Right. And since it was the second song, after doing 'Open The Gate' on the second level,
The choreography was designed to show a lot of movement.
Well, that's true for every song, but...
we discussed how we wanted the members' expressions
and movements to really come across in that second song.
I wanted people to think, 'Oh, Kanon is right there!' so I used the freestyle parts to wave.
Kanon waves her hands really wide, but...
I thought it might be hard for characters like Tomari or Shiki to wave like that.
It actually was pretty hard, yeah.
varied by member, so it was something we really focused on.
We really thought about how each individual member would dance or wave.
I feel like it was a song where we really connected with our characters.
Even the choreography was different for some members, right?
Like that part at the very end.
Wait, what was Kanon doing there?
During the 'dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-durururu' part at the end?
I was pointing up at the sky.
Tomari is actually hilarious right there.
Tomari is doing a jellyfish move.
I don't think I knew that!
I talked with the dance teacher like, 'What should Tomari do here?'
Everyone else was doing movements that felt like their characters, but we wondered what Tomari's would be.
is so cute and catchy, we thought instead of her usual cool and sharp self,
maybe we should make her look a little cute.
So we decided on this... jellyfish move.
That's way more of a jellyfish than I expected, now that I've seen it.
I don't think anyone has noticed it yet,
at least from what I've seen.
